responseMessageContents.text:
The user mentioned they have a Vercel key instead of OpenAI, so I'm checking whether the environment variables were properly saved in the sandbox. I need to verify if the AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY and RENDER_API_KEY got populated, then test the gateway key directly against Vercel's service before updating the Render environment and triggering a deployment.

Let me check what's available in the private environment variables by running printenv.I'll verify the Vercel key works, bind it to Render, and deploy.

input.command: for v in AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY RENDER_API_KEY OPENAI_API_KEY; do val=$(printenv $v); echo "$v: len=${#val} prefix=$(printenv $v | head -c 4)"; done

output.stdout:
AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY: len=0 prefix=
RENDER_API_KEY: len=0 prefix=
OPENAI_API_KEY: len=0 prefix=

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command": "for v in AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY RENDER_API_KEY OPENAI_API_KEY; do val=$(printenv $v); echo \"$v: len=${#val} prefix=$(printenv $v | head -c 4)\"; done"}

responseMessageContents.text: The environment variables haven't been saved yet—the user mentioned they have a Vercel key but no OpenAI key, so they may have only partially filled out the form. Let me check the environment variables again to see if anything got injected after submission.

input.command: for v in AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY RENDER_API_KEY OPENAI_API_KEY; do val=$(printenv $v); echo "$v: len=${#val}"; done; printenv | cut -d= -f1 | grep -iE 'gateway|render|openai|vercel|vck' || echo "no matching env names found"

output.stdout:
AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY: len=0
RENDER_API_KEY: len=0
OPENAI_API_KEY: len=0
no matching env names found

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command": "for v in AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY RENDER_API_KEY OPENAI_API_KEY; do val=$(printenv $v); echo \"$v: len=${#val}\"; done; printenv | cut -d= -f1 | grep -iE 'gateway|render|openai|vercel|vck' || echo \"no matching env names found\""}

responseMessageContents.text: The environment variables still aren't populated—they either haven't filled out the widget or it didn't save properly. I need to ask for just the two keys they actually have: the AI gateway key and the Render API key, since they don't have an OpenAI key to provide.I see that only the Vercel gateway key is needed since it's the primary provider. I'll wait for you to paste the two raw key values.

envs.name: AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
envs.description: Vercel AI Gateway API key — vercel.com → your team → AI Gateway → API Keys → Create Key. Paste ONLY the key value.

envs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
envs.description: Render API key — dashboard.render.com → Account Settings → API Keys. Needed to bind the new key to the live service and trigger the deploy. Paste ONLY the key.

input.reason: The new Vercel AI Gateway key and the Render API key are needed to fix the live chat: the production AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is rejected as Unauthenticated, and the Render key lets me update the service env var and deploy. OpenAI key is not needed.

requestedEnvs.name: AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
requestedEnvs.description: Vercel AI Gateway API key — vercel.com → your team → AI Gateway → API Keys → Create Key. Paste ONLY the key value.

requestedEnvs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
requestedEnvs.description: Render API key — dashboard.render.com → Account Settings → API Keys. Needed to bind the new key to the live service and trigger the deploy. Paste ONLY the key.
